name: Hybrid identity environment — corp.local / corp.com
description: A mid-sized organization with hybrid identity: an on-premises Active Directory domain (corp.local) — a perimeter firewall, a domain controller, and staff workstations — synced via Microsoft Entra Connect to a Microsoft Entra ID / Microsoft 365 tenant (corp.com) comprising Exchange Online, SharePoint Online, and a GRC/ITSM platform used for change management and engagement records.

segments.description: On-premises corp.local Active Directory domain and LAN. Address range 10.10.0.0/16.
The corp.com Microsoft Entra ID / Microsoft 365 tenant, hybrid-joined to corp.local via Microsoft Entra Connect.

devices.notes: Internet-facing edge device for the on-premises network. NATs outbound traffic and provides a VPN gateway for remote staff access to on-prem resources.

Primary Active Directory domain controller for corp.local. Also runs DNS for the internal network. Synced to the corp.com Microsoft Entra ID tenant via Microsoft Entra Connect (hybrid identity) — most on-prem accounts have a corresponding cloud identity.
Primary workstation for an IT administrator (jdoe) who handles day-to-day Microsoft 365 / Entra ID administration — app registrations, licensing, and access requests — alongside on-prem AD tasks.

devices.role: cloud identity provider and M365 admin center
devices.notes: Identity provider for the corp.com Microsoft 365 tenant, hybrid-joined to corp.local via Microsoft Entra Connect. Exposes interactive and service-principal sign-in logs, Entra ID audit logs (account, application, and consent changes), Identity Protection risk detections, conditional access policies, and the tenant's app/service-principal registry (registrations, delegated and application permission grants, consents). Roughly 150 user accounts, including staff, IT/admin accounts, service accounts, and a number of external/guest and contractor accounts (e.g. for auditors, vendors, and consultants) granted for the duration of their engagements.
Hosts mailboxes for all corp.com users, including the executive team and staff working on an active M&A workstream. Supports mailbox-level audit logging (e.g. MailItemsAccessed) and message tracing.
Hosts departmental and project SharePoint sites, including sites supporting the M&A workstream referenced above. Site-level access is logged via the SharePoint access/audit log.

devices.role: change management, ticketing, and vendor/engagement records
devices.notes: System of record for change tickets, security engagement records (e.g. third-party assessments/penetration tests), and vendor/contractor access requests across both the on-prem and cloud environments.
